habitat: Mountain broadleaf/mixed forest; forest edge; parks; gardens; and wooded areas
story: The Green Backed Flycatcher (Ficedula elisae) is an extraordinary bird that belongs to the Muscicapidae family. It is most commonly observed in Mountain broadleaf/mixed forest; forest edge; parks; gardens; and wooded areas, where its behaviors contribute to the ecological dynamics of the area. Listed as Least Concern (LC) by the IUCN, this species' future depends on continued conservation action. The story of the Green Backed Flycatcher is one of adaptation and resilience, offering valuable lessons for conservationists. This poster aims to bring the Green Backed Flycatcher into focus, highlighting its beauty and the importance of its habitat.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
